mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress multiple expansion, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, with Rank IC exceeding 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market cooling and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of periods where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with expanding industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS and INDPRO move in opposite directions, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 across all interaction quadrants.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns during high-inflation periods, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.8% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and higher household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months with payroll growth, or the correlation coefficient between payroll changes and 20-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month consecutive decline in the index of industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening business investment, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when the decline is driven by temporary supply chain disruptions rather than demand weakness, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside increasing federal funds rates indicate that monetary tightening is successfully cooling the housing sector, which reduces construction-related earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting returns, or positive returns dominate when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% quarter-over-quarter indicates weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, which depresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when GDP growth slows below 1.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy is indistinguishable from random chance with Rank IC below 0.01.
